summary: First testing run — bilingual book-launch site (Arundhati Roy — 'मेरी माँ मेरी गैंगस्टर / Mother Mary Comes To Me'). Backend (9/9 pytest cases) and frontend (all critical flows) fully passed. Verified: hero content (bilingual titles, ₹1499 price, cover image), nav & hero Reserve CTAs smooth-scroll to form, copies stepper clamped to 1..3, live total = copies × 1499, POST /api/reservations returns edition number, success block renders with correct edition and paid amount, /api/inventory decrements accordingly, and Edition section shows the same remaining count on reload. Validation errors (invalid email / missing fields / copies out of range) return 4xx as expected.

minor.issue: Returns 200 OK for a resource-create instead of 201 Created (non-blocking; standard REST convention).

Edition number is computed as reserved+1 with no DB transaction. Under concurrent requests two clients could theoretically receive the same edition number (currently mitigated only by aggregate count). Add unique index / atomic counter if scaling.

issues:
The Hindi title 'मेरी माँ' and 'मेरी गैंगस्टर' use a
so the visual line-break renders fine, but text_content concatenates them as 'मेरी माँमेरी गैंगस्टर' (no space). Purely a semantic/a11y observation — copy is fine visually.

Inline style fontFamily: 'Google Sans, cursive' with fontStyle italic — Google Sans is not cursive, so this falls back to serif italic. Consider using a proper handwritten/signature font (e.g. 'Great Vibes', 'Homemade Apple') for the signature aesthetic.

action_items: (Optional) Return 201 Created from POST /api/reservations for REST correctness.
(Optional / future scale) Prevent duplicate edition numbers under concurrent POSTs by using an atomic MongoDB counter (findOneAndUpdate on a counters collection) or a unique index on edition_number.
(Optional design) Use a script/handwriting webfont for the 'Arundhati Roy' signature in Edition.jsx — currently Google Sans italic which does not look like a signature.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: /api/reservations manually stringifies created_at before insert — fine, but consider letting MongoDB store native BSON datetime for future querying.
server.py: Concurrency risk — edition_number derived from aggregate count is not atomic. For a limited 1500-copy edition this could produce duplicates under simultaneous submissions.
App.js loadInventory swallows errors silently — acceptable for MVP but consider surfacing an error state so remaining count doesn't stay stale at defaults if the API is down.
ReservationForm.jsx: No client-side email/phone validation before submit; relies fully on backend 422. Works, but a small local check would improve UX.
Hero.jsx hardcodes 'Edition 1 / {total}' in the stats strip — misleading label since it always shows '1'. Consider removing or wiring to next available edition number from inventory.
test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created a few reservations with TEST_ prefixed data (full_name/email/address). Not cleaned up (no DELETE endpoint exists). Reserved count grew by ~7 during testing (still 1493 remaining, well under the 1500 cap).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 9 backend tests + full UI flow (nav CTA scroll, hero CTA scroll, copies stepper clamp, form submit, success block, /api/inventory decrement, reload check) pass. Testing added ~7 reservations in the DB (edition numbers up through 7). Backend is missing a DELETE / admin cleanup endpoint; if you need a pristine inventory count for future tests, either drop the 'reservations' collection or expose a cleanup route. No blocking issues; only optional REST-correctness (201) and concurrency-safety improvements were noted.
